turns.content: I've read about hiding new() and using things like Foo::empty() or from_parts(), but I still don't fully understand why we should replace the generic constructor with these named methods in our project.

The core reason is that named constructors guide users toward creating objects only through safe, validated paths. When you expose Foo::empty() and Foo::from_parts(), you are essentially implementing a trait like Default or FromIterator for your type, which ensures the object is always in a valid state without requiring manual field initialization via unsafe code. This approach makes your API self-documenting because the method name tells the developer exactly what semantic value they expect to receive, whereas new() just returns raw memory that could be corrupted if initialized incorrectly.
